coreIssue: This 1:1 is likely about whether a junior Product Designer three months in feels clear, supported, and steady amid shifting requirements.
openingQuestion: What feels hardest to navigate in your work right now when priorities or requirements shift, and where would more clarity or support help most?
listenFor: whether he names a specific recent project where the goalposts changed
whether he hesitates or stays vague when describing what good junior-level work looks like
if they mention holding questions back, waiting too long, or worrying about slowing others down
avoid: do not jump straight into reassurance before you hear concrete examples
do not turn the conversation into a performance readout or defend the team's process too early
goodOutcome: You and Kenji have agreed one concrete definition of what good junior Product Designer work looks like on his current scope, plus one clear point where he should ask for help earlier.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one current or recent piece of Kenji's work where shifting requirements were visible so you can test whether the confusion is about expectations, support, or churn.
